articles.title: Tesla Mulls Developing Affordable, Compact EV Amid Sales Revival: Report
articles.summary: Tesla is reportedly considering developing an affordable, compact electric vehicle, signaling a potential shift back to human-driven cars and an expansion of its product line. This comesamid recent sales shortfalls and the apparent discontinuation of the Model S and Model X, with the company also working on a "Cybercab" model. The new compact EV could be produced in China and priced below the Model 3 to boost sales.

articles.title: Gary Black Says Tesla's 8-Week Slide Driven By 'Disappointing' Deliveries, Robotaxi Doubts: 'EVs Still Comprise 70%...'
articles.summary: Investor Gary Black attributes Tesla's eight-week stock slide to "disappointing" first-quarter delivery figures and overzealous investor expectations for its robotaxi business. Black emphasizes that electric vehicles still account for 70% of Tesla's profits and criticizes the company's marketing strategy for Full Self-Driving (FSD). Additionally, the article notes a significant surplus of unsold Tesla vehicles, alongside news of two new models in development.

articles.title: Cathie Wood Doubles Down On Tesla Amid 60% Downside Warning: Ark Adds Another $11.4 Million Worth Of TSLA
articles.summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has continued to invest heavily in Tesla (TSLA), purchasing an additional $11.4 million worth of shares, bringing this week's total to $27.8 million. This comes despite a JPMorgan analyst's warning of a potential 60% downside due to increasing unsold vehicle inventory. Ark Invest maintains its long-term bullish outlook on Tesla, reinforced by a recent favorable regulatory decision.

articles.title: GLJ Research cuts Tesla stock price target citing options market shift
articles.summary: GLJ Research reiterated a "Sell" rating for Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and maintained a $25.28 price target, significantly below its current trading price of $343.25. The firm attributes Tesla's historical share price elevation to a retail call-buying dependent options market structure that has now broken down in 2026, removing a significant mechanical tailwind. Consequently, GLJ Research anticipates material downside risk as Tesla's valuation re-rates towards fundamentals without the previous options market support.

articles.title: Down More Than 30% from All-Time Highs, Should You Buy the Tesla Stock Dip?
articles.summary: Tesla stock has fallen over 30% from its December highs, making investors question its valuation ahead of the Q1 earnings report. Despite a high forward P/E, the article argues that Tesla's vast data from its vehicles and its focus on autonomy and AI justify the premium, offering a buying opportunity. Analysts maintain a "Buy" rating, citing strong demand and future growth potential in new technologies.

Analysts maintain a "Buy" rating, citing strong demand and future growth potential in new technologies.

articles.title: Rivian Stock Spikes Nearly 3% After-Hours: What's Going On?
articles.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc. (NASDAQ: RIVN) shares rose nearly 3% after hours following the discovery of an EPA certification for its R2 mid-size electric SUV. The filing suggests the R2's range could reach 335 miles, improving its competitive stance against the Tesla Model Y. Despite an "Outperform" rating from Baird and reaffirming its full-year delivery target, the stock shows short-term bearish technical indicators and a mixed analyst outlook, with its next earnings report due April 30, 2026.

articles.title: Tesla's lower-cost EV plan seen boosting volume, risking margins
articles.summary: Tesla's plan to develop a smaller, lower-cost EV is expected to boost sales and address slowing demand, especially in competitive markets like China. However, investors and analysts warn that this strategy could further strain Tesla's already pressured profit margins. The move comes as Tesla faces increasing competition and weakening demand, indicated by a significant gap between production and deliveries.
